content: We can reframe the task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as the base backbone for multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where each iteration of self-feedback is seeded by a distinct inconsistent problem-solving perspective rather than random re-sampling. This creates a hierarchical reflection structure that improves output quality without extra training, while addressing the limitation of vanilla Self-Refine’s tendency to get stuck in local optima from repeated single-perspective feedback.
